model.label: Qwen 3 Max
model.description: Alibaba • Suitable for complex tasks, with the strongest capabilities.

pick.rationale: Michigan State is the stronger team based on its Big Ten pedigree, superior overall record (25–7 vs. 27–7), and experience in high-stakes games under coach Tom Izzo. While North Dakota State enters on a four-game win streak after sweeping the Summit League titles, the Bison haven’t faced competition comparable to the Spartans’ schedule. No major injuries are reported for either side, but Michigan State’s physicality, defensive discipline, and tournament-tested roster give them a clear edge in this 3/14 matchup.

reasoningStep.title: Final Analysis: Team Quality, Schedule, and Form
reasoningStep.summary: Evaluated team records, conference strength, recent form, and injury status to determine the favorite.
evidence: Michigan State finished 25–7 in the Big Ten; North Dakota State went 27–7 and won both Summit League regular season and tournament titles.
MSU has lost two straight games but has extensive NCAA tournament experience; NDSU is on a four-game winning streak but against weaker competition.
No significant injuries reported for either team based on available news sources.
Historical trends strongly favor 3-seeds over 14-seeds, especially when the higher seed comes from a power conference like the Big Ten.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
Bracket config id: demo-2026-bracket
Tournament year: 2026
Current game id: r64-east-6
Current game label: East 3/14
Round: Round of 64
Resolved teams: Michigan State (michigan-state) vs North Dakota State (north-dakota-state)
Prior committed picks: 9
